clusters.title: What we know about the suspect in shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner
clusters.neutral_headline: What we're learning about suspected Washington press dinner gunman
bullet_summary: The word “staged” exploded on social media following the attack, as both right- and left-wing influencers and anonymous accounts spread unfounded conspiracy theories
Reported by CBS News, NBC World, Financial Times and others

items.title: What we know about the suspect in shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner
items.standfirst: The suspect was identified to CBS News by law enforcement sources as 31-year-old Cole Allen of Torrance, California.
items.content: The suspect was identified to CBS News by law enforcement sources as 31-year-old Cole Allen of Torrance, California.

items.title: Suspect charged with assassination attempt in correspondents' dinner shooting
items.standfirst: Cole Allen was charged Monday in federal court with three counts including attempt to assassinate the president.
items.content: Cole Allen was charged Monday in federal court with three counts including attempt to assassinate the president.

items.title: Trump asked about conspiracy theory that shooting was staged
items.standfirst: Even as the Justice Department gathers evidence, some corners of the internet are brewing with conspiracies about the attack at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. In her interview Sunday with President Trump, "60 Minutes" contributing correspondent Norah O'Donnell asked the president about those theories.
items.content: Even as the Justice Department gathers evidence, some corners of the internet are brewing with conspiracies about the attack at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. In her interview Sunday with President Trump, "60 Minutes" contributing correspondent Norah O'Donnell asked the president about those theories.

items.title: Correspondents' dinner shooting suspect charged with trying to assassinate the president
items.standfirst: Two days after allegedly attempting to charge into the White House Correspondents' Dinner with a gun on Saturday night, Cole Allen was in a federal courtroom Monday facing formal Department of Justice charges of the attempted assassination of a president. Matt Gutman reports.
items.content: Two days after allegedly attempting to charge into the White House Correspondents' Dinner with a gun on Saturday night, Cole Allen was in a federal courtroom Monday facing formal Department of Justice charges of the attempted assassination of a president. Matt Gutman reports.

items.title: Ex-Secret Service deputy director breaks down security response to correspondents' dinner shooting
items.standfirst: The Trump administration has ordered a review of its security protocols after Saturday's shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. A. T. Smith, former deputy director of the U.S. Secret Service, joins CBS News with analysis.
items.content: The Trump administration has ordered a review of its security protocols after Saturday's shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. A. T. Smith, former deputy director of the U.S. Secret Service, joins CBS News with analysis.

items.title: Why the planned White House ballroom couldn't host correspondents' dinner
items.standfirst: President Trump has used Saturday's shooting to renew his push for his presidential ballroom and East Wing renovations at the White House. CBS News' Lindsey Reiser takes a look at some of the reasons why the white house may not be an appropriate venue to hold the correspondents' dinner.
items.content: President Trump has used Saturday's shooting to renew his push for his presidential ballroom and East Wing renovations at the White House. CBS News' Lindsey Reiser takes a look at some of the reasons why the white house may not be an appropriate venue to hold the correspondents' dinner.

items.title: CBS News analyst describes security concerns he had prior to correspondents' dinner shooting
items.standfirst: The man accused of rushing the White House Correspondents' Dinner armed with a shotgun, pistol and three knives has been charged with trying to assassinate President Trump. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean, who attended the dinner, joins to describe what he witnessed.
items.content: The man accused of rushing the White House Correspondents' Dinner armed with a shotgun, pistol and three knives has been charged with trying to assassinate President Trump. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean, who attended the dinner, joins to describe what he witnessed.

items.title: New details on suspect's movements ahead of correspondents' dinner shooting
items.standfirst: We are learning more about the suspect and his movements ahead of Saturday night's alleged assassination attempt of President Trump at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the details.
items.content: We are learning more about the suspect and his movements ahead of Saturday night's alleged assassination attempt of President Trump at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the details.

items.title: Trump administration blames heightened political rhetoric for correspondents' dinner shooting
items.standfirst: The shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner is shedding light on the increase in political rhetoric and violence in the U.S. Trump administration officials have pointed the finger at Democrats and the media. CBS News political director Fin Gómez joins with analysis.
items.content: The shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner is shedding light on the increase in political rhetoric and violence in the U.S. Trump administration officials have pointed the finger at Democrats and the media. CBS News political director Fin Gómez joins with analysis.

items.title: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive in Washington to meet with President Trump and First Lady
items.standfirst: England’s king and queen are in the US as part of a reset with the president after recent tensions with the UK surrounding the war in Iran. The royal couple is here to celebrate America’s 250th anniversary. NBC News’ Hallie Jackson has the latest on their visit
items.content: England’s king and queen are in the US as part of a reset with the president after recent tensions with the UK surrounding the war in Iran. The royal couple is here to celebrate America’s 250th anniversary. NBC News’ Hallie Jackson has the latest on their visit

items.title: Inside the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
items.standfirst: Prosecutors on Monday charged the man accused of opening fire at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday with three counts, including the attempted assassination of President Trump. CBS News' Jake Rosen, Nicole Sganga and Aaron Navarro have the latest.
items.content: Prosecutors on Monday charged the man accused of opening fire at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday with three counts, including the attempted assassination of President Trump. CBS News' Jake Rosen, Nicole Sganga and Aaron Navarro have the latest.

items.title: King Charles, Queen Camilla arrive in Washington, D.C., for state visit
items.standfirst: King Charles is now in Washington, D.C., for the first state visit by a British monarch in almost 20 years. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns has the latest.
items.content: King Charles is now in Washington, D.C., for the first state visit by a British monarch in almost 20 years. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns has the latest.

items.title: Correspondents' dinner shooting suspect charged with attempting to assassinate president
items.standfirst: The suspected gunman in Saturday's attack at the White House Correspondents' Dinner appeared in court on Monday and faced a new charge: attempted assassination of the U.S. president. CBS News' Nicole Sganga, Ed O'Keefe and Aaron MacLean have the latest.
items.content: The suspected gunman in Saturday's attack at the White House Correspondents' Dinner appeared in court on Monday and faced a new charge: attempted assassination of the U.S. president. CBS News' Nicole Sganga, Ed O'Keefe and Aaron MacLean have the latest.

items.title: ‘Staged’: Conspiracy theories surge after shooting at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
items.standfirst: Social media was flooded with conspiracy theories after a gunman's thwarted attack at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday, with both left-wing and right-wing accounts claiming - without evidence - that the attack was "staged." By midday Sunday, the term "staged" appeared in over 300,000 posts on X, according to social media analytics firm TweetBinder.
items.content: Social media was flooded with conspiracy theories after a gunman's thwarted attack at the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday, with both left-wing and right-wing accounts claiming - without evidence - that the attack was "staged." By midday Sunday, the term "staged" appeared in over 300,000 posts on X, according to social media analytics firm TweetBinder.

items.title: The King Arrives In The US, But Can He Mend The Special Relationship?
items.standfirst: The King lands in Washington for four day state visit.
items.content: The King lands in Washington for four day state visit.

items.title: Suspect in press gala shooting charged with attempted assassination of Trump
items.standfirst: The man suspected of having opened fire at the annual correspondents’ dinner in Washington over the weekend was on Monday charged with the attempted assassination of US President Donald Trump. The man, who managed to bypass security and fire several shots before he was wrestled down, had brought several weapons with him, including a pump gun and three knives.
items.content: The man suspected of having opened fire at the annual correspondents’ dinner in Washington over the weekend was on Monday charged with the attempted assassination of US President Donald Trump. The man, who managed to bypass security and fire several shots before he was wrestled down, had brought several weapons with him, including a pump gun and three knives.

items.title: Culture of hate? Trump's America and the White House press gala shooting
items.standfirst: Even if it's a ‘lone wolf’ as first suggested by President Trump, Saturday night’s White House Correspondents Dinner drama certainly did not happen in a vacuum. And just as mass shootings have become commonplace in a United States overrun by firearms so have attempts on the lives of political figures. We’ll ask about the latest targeting of the US president and the echo chambers that amplify vilification and hate, that turn adversaries into enemies, a social media landscape where conspiracy theories can make a better fit than facts both as ideology and a business model.
items.content: Even if it's a ‘lone wolf’ as first suggested by President Trump, Saturday night’s White House Correspondents Dinner drama certainly did not happen in a vacuum. And just as mass shootings have become commonplace in a United States overrun by firearms so have attempts on the lives of political figures. We’ll ask about the latest targeting of the US president and the echo chambers that amplify vilification and hate, that turn adversaries into enemies, a social media landscape where conspiracy theories can make a better fit than facts both as ideology and a business model.

items.title: Carney announces Canada’s first sovereign wealth fund
items.standfirst: Prime Minister Mark Carney says the new Canada Strong Fund will be Canada’s first sovereign wealth fund, with an initial federal contribution of $25 billion. It is intended to invest alongside the private sector in major projects and companies in areas such as energy, infrastructure, mining, agriculture and technology. [cbc.ca]
items.content: Prime Minister Mark Carney says the new Canada Strong Fund will be Canada’s first sovereign wealth fund, with an initial federal contribution of $25 billion. It is intended to invest alongside the private sector in major projects and companies in areas such as energy, infrastructure, mining, agriculture and technology. [cbc.ca]

items.title: Bomb blast on Colombia highway leaves 21 dead amid pre-election violence
items.standfirst: Cocaine-trafficking rebels blamed for worst attack on civilians in decades, which also left 56 people injuredThe death toll in a Colombian highway bombing blamed on cocaine-trafficking rebels has risen to 21, the government said on Monday, in the country’s worst attack on civilians in decades and just ahead of elections.The attack on Saturday left 56 injured and buses and vans mangled on the Pan-American Highway, in the restive south-western Cauca department. Continue reading...

items.title: Taylor Swift files trademarks for voice and image amid concern over AI misuse
items.standfirst: The singer’s company filed three applications on Friday after Matthew McConaughey launched similar strategyTaylor Swift has filed applications to trademark her voice and image in a move seemingly designed to protect against AI misuse.On 24 April, Swift’s company TAS Rights Management filed three trademark applications, Variety reports. Two of these are sound trademarks that cover Swift saying the phrases “Hey, it’s Taylor Swift” and “Hey, it’s Taylor.” Continue reading...
items.content: The singer’s company filed three applications on Friday after Matthew McConaughey launched similar strategyTaylor Swift has filed applications to trademark her voice and image in a move seemingly designed to protect against AI misuse.On 24 April, Swift’s company TAS Rights Management filed three trademark applications, Variety reports. Two of these are sound trademarks that cover Swift saying the phrases “Hey, it’s Taylor Swift” and “Hey, it’s Taylor.” Continue reading...

clusters.title: Google Staff Urge Pichai to Refuse Classified Military AI Work
clusters.neutral_headline: Google Staff Urge Pichai to Refuse Classified Military AI Work
bullet_summary: Reported by Bloomberg Technology, Financial Times

items.title: Google Staff Urge Pichai to Refuse Classified Military AI Work
items.standfirst: Hundreds of AI researchers at Alphabet Inc.’s Google have signed onto a letter urging Chief Executive Officer Sundar Pichai to refuse to make the company’s artificial intelligence systems available for classified workloads for US defense missions, according to organizers of the effort.
items.content: Hundreds of AI researchers at Alphabet Inc.’s Google have signed onto a letter urging Chief Executive Officer Sundar Pichai to refuse to make the company’s artificial intelligence systems available for classified workloads for US defense missions, according to organizers of the effort.

items.title: Google staff urge chief executive to block US military AI use
items.standfirst: Over 560 employees sign open letter to Sundar Pichai following the Pentagon’s clash with Anthropic
items.content: Over 560 employees sign open letter to Sundar Pichai following the Pentagon’s clash with Anthropic

clusters.title: King Charles heads to Washington on a mission to restore UK-US relationship
clusters.neutral_headline: King Charles heads to Washington on a mission to restore UK-US relationship
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, NBC World

items.title: King Charles heads to Washington on a mission to restore UK-US relationship
items.standfirst: Two and a half centuries after the American colonies declared independence from Britain under King George III, his descendant King Charles III lands in Washington on Monday with trans-Atlantic ties under strain and security in the spotlight. France 24's Philip Turle takes a look at the agenda and what might be at stake.
items.content: Two and a half centuries after the American colonies declared independence from Britain under King George III, his descendant King Charles III lands in Washington on Monday with trans-Atlantic ties under strain and security in the spotlight. France 24's Philip Turle takes a look at the agenda and what might be at stake.

items.title: King Charles' royal charm offensive seeks to win over Trump and reset a souring relationship
items.standfirst: More than two centuries after the 13 colonies rebelled against King George III and sent his redcoats packing, a direct descendant of that monarch will arrive Monday in Washington to mark the 250th anniversary of American independence from British rule
items.content: More than two centuries after the 13 colonies rebelled against King George III and sent his redcoats packing, a direct descendant of that monarch will arrive Monday in Washington to mark the 250th anniversary of American independence from British rule

clusters.title: Archbishop praises Pope's anti-war comments during Vatican visit
clusters.neutral_headline: Archbishop praises Pope's anti-war comments during Vatican visit
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Archbishop praises Pope's anti-war comments during Vatican visit
items.standfirst: Dame Sarah Mullally, the first female head of the Church of England, had a private discussion with Pope Leo during the trip.
items.content: Dame Sarah Mullally, the first female head of the Church of England, had a private discussion with Pope Leo during the trip.

items.title: Archbishop of Canterbury prays with Pope Leo in historic meeting
items.standfirst: Dame Sarah Mullally, the first female Archbishop of Canterbury, has prayed with Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican - a meeting that would have been unthinkable just a few years ago.
items.content: Dame Sarah Mullally, the first female Archbishop of Canterbury, has prayed with Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican - a meeting that would have been unthinkable just a few years ago.

clusters.title: Kim Jong Un speaks at memorial for North Korean soldiers killed fighting for Russia
clusters.neutral_headline: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
bullet_summary: Reported by Sky News World, BBC World

items.title: Kim Jong Un speaks at memorial for North Korean soldiers killed fighting for Russia
items.standfirst: Kim Jong Un threw dirt over the remains of one dead soldier at the opening of a memorial museum for his compatriots who died fighting alongside Russia in the Ukraine war.
items.content: Kim Jong Un threw dirt over the remains of one dead soldier at the opening of a memorial museum for his compatriots who died fighting alongside Russia in the Ukraine war.

items.title: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
items.standfirst: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.
items.content: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.

clusters.title: ‘Israel must change direction’: Netanyahu rivals join forces for next election
clusters.neutral_headline: ‘Israel must change direction’: Netanyahu rivals join forces for next election
bullet_summary: Two of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's biggest rivals have announced they are joining forces to attempt to oust his coalition government
Reported by Guardian World, Sky News World

items.title: ‘Israel must change direction’: Netanyahu rivals join forces for next election
items.standfirst: Rightwing Naftali Bennett and centrist Yair Lapid announce new party before Knesset vote expected later this yearThe Israeli prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, is facing the prospect of running against a rightwing-centrist super coalition in elections later this year after two of his most formidable political rivals combined forces in an attempt to oust him, inviting a third party leader to join them.In a move that some analysts compared to the centre-right coalition that removed Viktor Orbán from power in Hungary, the former prime ministers – rightwing Naftali Bennett and centrist Yair Lapid – issued statements announcing the merger of their parties, Bennett 2026 and Yesh Atid (There is a Future). Continue reading...
items.content: Rightwing Naftali Bennett and centrist Yair Lapid announce new party before Knesset vote expected later this yearThe Israeli prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, is facing the prospect of running against a rightwing-centrist super coalition in elections later this year after two of his most formidable political rivals combined forces in an attempt to oust him, inviting a third party leader to join them.In a move that some analysts compared to the centre-right coalition that removed Viktor Orbán from power in Hungary, the former prime ministers – rightwing Naftali Bennett and centrist Yair Lapid – issued statements announcing the merger of their parties, Bennett 2026 and Yesh Atid (There is a Future). Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Two of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's biggest rivals have announced they are joining forces to attempt to oust his coalition government.
items.content: Two of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's biggest rivals have announced they are joining forces to attempt to oust his coalition government.
